sections.label: Section 538
sections.original: ὃς δ ’ ἂν μεθυσθείς γ ’ ἐν δόμοις μείνῃ , σοφός .
sections.translation: But whoso stays at home , when drunk , is wise .

sections.label: Section 539
sections.original: τί δρῶμεν , ὦ Σιληνέ ; σοὶ μένειν δοκεῖ ;
sections.translation: What shall we do , Silenus ? art minded to stay ?

sections.label: Section 540
sections.original: δοκεῖ . τί γὰρ δεῖ συμποτῶν ἄλλων , Κύκλωψ ;
sections.translation: That I am ; for what need have we of others to share our drink , Cyclops ?

sections.label: Section 541
sections.original: καὶ μὴν λαχνῶδές τ ’ οὖδας ἀνθηρᾶς χλόης . . . .
sections.translation: Well , truly the turf is soft as down with its fresh flowering plants .

sections.label: Section 542
sections.original: καὶ πρός γε θάλπος ἡλίου πίνειν καλόν .
sections.translation: ( seating himself .) Aye , and ’ tis pleasant drinking in the warm sunshine .

sections.label: Section 543
sections.original: κλίθητί νύν μοι πλευρὰ θεὶς ἐπὶ χθονός .
sections.translation: Come , let me see thee stretch thy carcase on the ground .

sections.label: Section 544
sections.original: ἰδού .
sections.translation: ( sitting dawn .) There then !

sections.label: Section 545
sections.original: τί δῆτα τὸν κρατῆρ ’ ὄπισθέ μου τίθης ;
sections.translation: Why art thou putting the mixing-bowl behind me ?

sections.label: Section 546
sections.original: ὡς μὴ παριών τις καταβάλῃ .
sections.translation: That no one passing by may come upon it . [*]

sections.label: Section 546b
sections.original: πίνειν μὲν οὖν
sections.translation: Nay , but thy purpose is to drink upon the sly ; set it between us . ( To Odysseus .) Now tell me , stranger , by what name to call thee .

sections.label: Section 549
sections.original: Οὖτιν · χάριν δὲ τίνα λαβών σ ’ ἐπαινέσω ;
sections.translation: Noman . What boon shall I receive of thee to earn my thanks ?

sections.label: Section 550
sections.original: πάντων σ ’ ἑταίρων ὕστερον θοινάσομαι .
sections.translation: I will feast on thee last , after all thy comrades .

sections.label: Section 551
sections.original: καλόν γε τὸ γέρας τῷ ξένῳ δίδως , Κύκλωψ .
sections.translation: Fair indeed the honour thou bestowest on thy guest , sir Cyclops !

sections.label: Section 552
sections.original: οὗτος , τί δρᾷς ; τὸν οἶνον ἐκπίνεις λάθρᾳ ;
sections.translation: ( turning suddenly to Silenus .) Ho , sirrah ! what art thou about ? taking a stealthy pull at the wine ?

sections.label: Section 553
sections.original: οὔκ , ἀλλ ’ ἔμ ’ οὗτος ἔκυσεν , ὅτι καλὸν βλέπω .
sections.translation: No , but it kissed me for my good looks .

sections.label: Section 554
sections.original: κλαύσῃ , φιλῶν τὸν οἶνον οὐ φιλοῦντά σε .
sections.translation: Thou shalt smart , if thou kiss the wine when it kisses not thee .

sections.label: Section 555
sections.original: ναὶ μὰ Δί ’, ἐπεί μού φησ ’ ἐρᾶν ὄντος καλοῦ .
sections.translation: Oh ! but it did , for it says it is in love with my handsome face .

sections.label: Section 556
sections.original: ἔγχει , πλέων δὲ τὸν σκύφον . δίδου μόνον .
sections.translation: ( holding out his cup .) Pour in ; only give me my cup full .

sections.label: Section 557
sections.original: πῶς οὖν κέκραται ; φέρε διασκεψώμεθα .
sections.translation: H ’ m ! how is it mixed ? just let me make sure . ( Takes another pull .)

sections.label: Section 558
sections.original: ἀπολεῖς · δὸς οὕτως .
sections.translation: Perdition ! give it me at once .

sections.label: Section 558b
sections.original: ναὶ μὰ Δί ’ οὐ πρὶν ἄν γέ σε
sections.translation: Oh , no ! I really cannot , till I see thee with a crown on , and have another taste myself .

sections.label: Section 560
sections.original: ὦ οἰνοχόος ἄδικος .
sections.translation: My cup-bearer is a cheat .

sections.label: Section 560b
sections.original: οὐ μὰ Δί ’, ἀλλ ’ ὦ οἶνος γλυκύς .
sections.translation: No really , but the wine is so luscious . Thou must wipe thy lips , though , to get a draught .

sections.label: Section 562
sections.original: ἰδού , καθαρὸν τὸ χεῖλος αἱ τρίχες τέ μου .
sections.translation: There ! my lips and beard are clean now .

sections.label: Section 563
sections.original: θές νυν τὸν ἀγκῶν ’ εὐρύθμως , κᾆτ ’ ἔκπιε ,
sections.translation: Bend thine elbow gracefully , and then quaff thy cup , as thou seest me do , and as now thou seest me not . ( Burying his face in his cup .)

sections.label: Section 565
sections.original: ἆ ἆ , τί δράσεις ;
sections.translation: Aha ! what next ?

sections.label: Section 565b
sections.original: ἡδέως ἠμύστισα .
sections.translation: I drank it off at a draught with much pleasure .

sections.label: Section 566
sections.original: λάβ ’, ὦ ξέν ’, αὐτὸς οἰνοχόος τέ μοι γενοῦ .
sections.translation: Stranger , take the skin thyself and be my cup-bearer .

sections.label: Section 567
sections.original: γιγνώσκεται γοῦν ἡ ἄμπελος τἠμῇ χερί .
sections.translation: Well , at any rate the grape is no stranger to my hand .

sections.label: Section 568
sections.original: φέρ ’ ἔγχεόν νυν .
sections.translation: Come , pour it in .

sections.label: Section 568b
sections.original: ἐγχέω , σίγα μόνον .
sections.translation: In it goes ! keep silence , that is all .

sections.label: Section 569
sections.original: χαλεπὸν τόδ ’ εἶπας , ὅστις ἂν πίνῃ πολύν .
sections.translation: A difficult task when a man is deep in his cups .

sections.label: Section 570
sections.original: ἰδοὺ λαβὼν ἔκπιθι καὶ μηδὲν λίπῃς .
sections.translation: Here , take and drink it off ; leave none .

sections.label: Section 571
sections.original: συνεκθανεῖν δὲ σπῶντα χρὴ τῷ πώματι .
sections.translation: Thou must be silent [*] and only give in when the liquor does .

sections.label: Section 572
sections.original: παπαῖ , σοφόν γε τὸ ξύλον τῆς ἀμπέλου .
sections.translation: God wot ! it is a clever stock that bears the grape .

sections.label: Section 573
sections.original: κἂν μὲν σπάσῃς γε δαιτὶ πρὸς πολλῇ πολύν ,
sections.translation: Aye , and if thou but swallow plenty of it after a plentiful meal , moistening thy belly till its thirst is gone , it will throw thee into slumber ;

sections.label: Section 575
sections.original: ἢν δ ’ ἐλλίπῃς τι , ξηρανεῖ σ ’ ὁ Βάκχιος .
sections.translation: but if thou leave aught behind , the Bacchic god will parch thee for it .

sections.label: Section 576
sections.original: ἰοὺ ἰού ,
sections.translation: Ha ! ha ! what a trouble it was getting out ! This is pleasure unalloyed ; earth and sky seem whirling round together ; I see the throne of Zeus

sections.label: Section 580
sections.original: λεύσσω , τὸ πᾶν τε δαιμόνων ἁγνὸν σέβας .
sections.translation: and all the godhead ’ s majesty . Kiss thee ! no ! There are the Graces trying to tempt me . I shall rest well enough with my Ganymede here ; yea , by the Graces , right fairly .

sections.label: Section 585
sections.original: ἐγὼ γὰρ ὁ Διός εἰμι Γανυμήδης , Κύκλωψ ;
sections.translation: What ! Cyclops , am I Ganymede , Zeus ’ s minion ?

sections.label: Section 586
sections.original: ναὶ μὰ Δί ’, ὃν ἁρπάζω γ ’ ἐγὼ ’ κ τοῦ Δαρδάνου .
sections.translation: ( attempting to carry him into the cave .) To be sure , Ganymede whom I am carrying off from the halls of Dardanus .

sections.label: Section 587
sections.original: ἀπόλωλα , παῖδες · σχέτλια πείσομαι κακά .
sections.translation: I am undone , my children ; outrageous treatment waits me .

sections.label: Section 588
sections.original: μέμφῃ τὸν ἐραστὴν κἀντρυφᾷς πεπωκότι ;
sections.translation: Dost find fault with thy lover ? dost scorn him in his cups ?

sections.label: Section 589
sections.original: οἴμοι · πικρότατον οἶνον ὄψομαι τάχα .
sections.translation: Woe is me ! most bitter shall I find the wine ere long . [ Exit Silenus , dragged away by Cyclops .

sections.label: Section 590
sections.original: ἄγε δή , Διονύσου παῖδες , εὐγενῆ τέκνα ,
sections.translation: Up now , children of Dionysus , sons of a noble sire , soon will yon creature in the cave , relaxed in slumber as ye see him , spew from his shameless maw the meat . Already the brand inside his lair is vomiting a cloud of smoke ; and the only reason we prepared it was to burn

sections.label: Section 595
sections.original: Κύκλωπος ὄψιν · ἀλλ ’ ὅπως ἀνὴρ ἔσῃ .
sections.translation: the Cyclops ’ eye ; so mind thou quit thee like a man .

sections.label: Section 596
sections.original: πέτρας τὸ λῆμα κἀδάμαντος ἕξομεν .
sections.translation: I will have a spirit as of rock or adamant ; but go inside , before my father suffers any shameful treatment ; for here thou hast things ready .

sections.label: Section 599
sections.original: Ἥφαιστ ’, ἄναξ Αἰτναῖε , γείτονος κακοῦ
sections.translation: O Hephaestus , lord of Aetna , rid thyself for once and all of a troublesome neighbour

sections.label: Section 600
sections.original: λαμπρὸν πυρώσας ὄμμ ’ ἀπαλλάχθηθ ’ ἅπαξ ,
sections.translation: by burning his bright eye out . Come , Sleep , as well , offspring of sable Night , come with all thy power on the monster god-detested ; and never after Troy ’ s most glorious toils destroy Odysseus and his crew

sections.label: Section 605
sections.original: ὑπ ’ ἀνδρός , ᾧ θεῶν οὐδὲν ἢ βροτῶν μέλει .
sections.translation: by the hands of one who recketh naught of God or man ; else must we reckon Chance a goddess , and Heaven ’ s wall inferior to hers . [ Odysseus re-enters the cave .

sections.label: Section 608
sections.original: λήψεται τὸν τράχηλον
sections.translation: Tightly the pincers shall grip the neck

sections.label: Section 610
sections.original: τοῦ ξενοδαιτυμόνος · πυρὶ γὰρ τάχα
sections.translation: of him who feasts upon his guest ; for soon will he lose the light of his eye by fire ; already the brand , a tree ’ s huge limb ,

sections.label: Section 615
sections.original: κρύπτεται ἐς σποδιάν , δρυὸς ἄσπετον
sections.translation: lurks amid the embers charred .

sections.label: Section 616
sections.original: ἔρνος · ἀλλ ’ ἴτω Μάρων ·
sections.translation: Oh ! come ye then and work his doom , pluck out the maddened Cyclops ’ eye , that he may rue his drinking .

sections.label: Section 620
sections.original: τὸν φιλοκισσοφόρον Βρόμιον πο-
sections.translation: And I too fain would leave the Cyclops ’ lonely land and see king Bromius , ivy-crowned , the god I sorely miss . Ah ! shall I ever come to that ?

sections.label: Section 624
sections.original: σιγᾶτε πρὸς θεῶν , θῆρες , ἡσυχάζετε ,
sections.translation: ( leaving the cave cautiously .) Silence , ye cattle ! I adjure you ;

sections.label: Section 625
sections.original: συνθέντες ἄρθρα στόματος · οὐδὲ πνεῖν ἐῶ ,
sections.translation: close your lips ; make not a sound ! I ’ ll not let a man of you so much as breathe or wink or clear his throat , that yon pest awake not , until the sight in the Cyclops ’ eye has passed through the fiery ordeal .

sections.label: Section 629
sections.original: σιγῶμεν ἐγκάψαντες αἰθέρα γνάθοις .
sections.translation: Silent we stand with ’ bated breath .

sections.label: Section 630
sections.original: ἄγε νυν ὅπως ἅψεσθε τοῦ δαλοῦ χεροῖν
sections.translation: In then , and mind your fingers grip the brand , for it is splendidly red-hot .

sections.label: Section 632
sections.original: οὐκοῦν σὺ τάξεις οὕστινας πρώτους χρεὼν
sections.translation: Thyself ordain who first must seize the blazing bar and burn the Cyclops ’ eye out , that we may share alike whate ’ er betides .
